title: psychotria spachiana
intro: Psychotria spachiana, also known as Spach's wild coffee or Psychotria, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Psychotria spachiana are thought to include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Traditionally, Psychotria spachiana has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used in folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
